questions.question: 1. Vermicular graphite is a characteristic of

explanations.a: This is an incorrect option. Grey cast iron contains graphite in flake form, not vermicular form.
explanations.b: This is an incorrect option. White cast iron contains cementite and has no free graphite.
explanations.c: This is an incorrect option. Ductile cast iron is characterized by spheroidal (nodular) graphite.
explanations.d: This is the correct option. Compacted cast iron is characterized by vermicular (worm-like) graphite morphology.

questions.question: 2. Eutectic reaction in the Fe-Fe<sub>3</sub>C phase diagram occurs at

explanations.a: This is an incorrect option. This is the melting temperature of pure iron, not a eutectic reaction.
explanations.b: This is an incorrect option. This temperature corresponds to the δ-ferrite to austenite transformation in iron.
explanations.c: This is the correct option. The eutectic reaction (Liquid → Austenite + Cementite, i.e., ledeburite) occurs at 1147 °C and 4.3 wt.%
explanations.d: This is an incorrect option. This is the eutectoid temperature where austenite transforms to pearlite.

questions.question: 3. Heavy machinery bases are made of

explanations.a: This is an incorrect option. White cast iron is very hard and brittle due to cementite and lacks the vibration-damping ability required for machinery bases.
explanations.b: This is the correct option. Grey cast iron has excellent vibration damping, good machinability, and high compressive strength, making it ideal for heavy machinery bases.
explanations.c: This is an incorrect option. Ductile iron has high strength and toughness but inferior vibration damping compared to grey cast iron.
explanations.d: This is an incorrect option. Malleable iron is tougher but does not provide the high damping capacity needed for heavy machinery foundations.

explanations.a: This is an incorrect option. Cast iron contains a high percentage of carbon (≈2–4%) and other impurities, so it is not pure iron.
explanations.b: This is the correct option. Wrought iron contains the lowest carbon content (≈0.02–0.08%) and is the purest commercial form of iron.
explanations.c: This is an incorrect option. Pig iron has very high carbon content (≈3.5–4.5%) and many impurities.
explanations.d: This is an incorrect option. Grey iron is a type of cast iron with flake graphite and significant carbon content.

questions.question: 5. Higher corrosion resistance can be obtained from cast iron due to which alloying addition.

explanations.a: This is an incorrect option. Carbon mainly influences hardness and microstructure but does not significantly improve corrosion resistance in cast iron.
explanations.b: This is an incorrect option. Manganese primarily improves strength and hardenability and has little role in enhancing corrosion resistance.
explanations.c: This is the correct option. Silicon improves corrosion resistance by promoting the formation of a protective silicon-rich oxide layer and stabilizing graphite in cast iron.
explanations.d: This is an incorrect option. Phosphorus improves fluidity and wear resistance but generally reduces toughness rather than corrosion resistance.
